questions.text: What will the disbelievers intensely wish for on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will wish that they had been Muslims.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the 'term' or lifespan of a nation?
questions.answer: No nation can precede its term, nor can they delay it; everything is decreed.

questions.text: What famous guarantee does Allah give regarding the Quran?
questions.answer: Indeed, it is We who sent down the message (Dhikr) and indeed, We will be its guardian.

questions.text: How did the disbelievers mock the Prophet (SAW) regarding his sanity?
questions.answer: They called him 'mad' (Majnun) and challenged him to bring angels if he was truthful.

questions.text: What is the reason Allah does not send down angels to the disbelievers?
questions.answer: He only sends them with the truth (for punishment or a final decree), and then the disbelievers would not be given any more time.

questions.text: What are the 'Buruj' mentioned in the sky?
questions.answer: Great constellations or stars that Allah has placed and beautified for the observers.

questions.text: How does Allah protect the heavens from every 'accused devil'?
questions.answer: By a piercing flame (Shihab) that pursues any devil who tries to eavesdrop on the celestial assembly.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the earth and its stability in this Surah?
questions.answer: He has spread it out, set firm mountains upon it, and caused everything to grow in its proper balance.

questions.text: What is the role of the 'fertilizing winds' (Lawaqih) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: They pollinate plants and clouds to bring down rain for people to drink and store.

questions.text: Does Allah possess the 'treasures' of all things?
questions.answer: Yes, there is not a thing but that with Him are its depositories, and He sends it down only in a known measure.

questions.text: From what material did Allah create humans?
questions.answer: From clay (Salsal) from altered black mud (Ham'-im-Masnun).

questions.text: From what were the Jinn created?
questions.answer: Before man, from scorching fire (Nar-is-Samum).

questions.text: What was the command given to the angels after the soul was breathed into Adam?
questions.answer: To fall down in prostration to him.

questions.text: Why did Iblis refuse to prostrate to Adam?
questions.answer: He argued he would not prostrate to a human created from clay and mud, showing his arrogance.

questions.text: What was the punishment for Iblis for his disobedience?
questions.answer: He was expelled from Paradise and cursed until the Day of Recompense.

questions.text: What respite did Allah grant Iblis when he asked for it?
questions.answer: He was granted a delay until the Day of the time well-known (Judgment Day).

questions.text: What did Iblis vow to do to mankind on earth?
questions.answer: To make disobedience and worldly life attractive to them and to mislead them all.

questions.text: Who are the only ones Iblis admitted he could not mislead?
questions.answer: The 'Mukhlaseen' (chosen/sincere servants of Allah).

questions.text: What is the 'Path' that leads to Allah as mentioned in this section?
questions.answer: The path of sincerity and devotion, over which Iblis has no authority.

questions.text: How many gates does Hell have?
questions.answer: Seven gates, with a specific group of followers of Iblis assigned to each.

questions.text: How are the believers in Paradise described in terms of their surroundings?
questions.answer: They will be in gardens and springs, entering them in peace and security.

questions.text: How is the state of the believers' hearts in Paradise described?
questions.answer: Allah will remove whatever resentment is in their breasts, making them brothers on thrones facing each other.

questions.text: What are the two major attributes of Allah that the Prophet is told to inform His servants about?
questions.answer: That He is the Forgiving, the Merciful, but also that His punishment is the most painful punishment.

questions.text: Briefly describe the visit of the angels to Ibrahim (AS).
questions.answer: They entered his home and said 'Salam'; he felt fear, but they told him not to be afraid as they had brought glad tidings of a learned son.

questions.text: What did the angels say to Ibrahim (AS) when he expressed amazement at having a child in old age?
questions.answer: They told him not to despair, for none despairs of the mercy of his Lord except the misguided.

questions.text: What was the 'mission' of the angels after visiting Ibrahim?
questions.answer: To destroy a people of criminals (the people of Lut).

questions.text: Who was the only family to be saved from the destruction of Lut's people?
questions.answer: The family of Lut, except for his wife.

questions.text: What was the decree regarding Lut's wife?
questions.answer: That she was among those destined to stay behind and be destroyed.

questions.text: How did the people of the city react when they heard about Lut's guests?
questions.answer: They came rejoicing, intending evil toward the guests.

questions.text: What was the final punishment for the people of Lut in this Surah?
questions.answer: A shriek (Saihah) seized them at sunrise, their city was turned upside down, and stones of baked clay rained upon them.

questions.text: Who were the 'People of the Wood' (Aykah)?
questions.answer: The people of Prophet Shu'ayb who were also wrongdoers and were destroyed by a punishment.

questions.text: Who were the 'People of Al-Hijr'?
questions.answer: The people of Thamud (Prophet Salih) who lived in the rocky tract.

questions.text: What were the houses of the people of Al-Hijr carved out of?
questions.answer: They carved their houses out of the mountains, feeling secure in their engineering.

questions.text: How was the punishment of the people of Al-Hijr delivered?
questions.answer: A shriek seized them in the morning, and their carvings and wealth did not avail them at all.

questions.text: With what purpose did Allah create the heavens and the earth?
questions.answer: With truth (purpose and justice).

questions.text: What is the 'Sab'an minal Mathani' (Seven of the oft-repeated) referred to in this Surah?
questions.answer: The seven verses of Surah Al-Fatiha.

questions.text: What is the instruction to the Prophet regarding the worldly wealth given to some disbelievers?
questions.answer: He is told not to extend his eyes toward it and not to grieve over them, but to be kind to the believers.

questions.text: What does the Surah say about those who 'divided the Quran' (Al-Muqtasimin)?
questions.answer: They are those who accepted some parts of revelation and rejected others; Allah swears He will question them all.

questions.text: What should the Prophet (SAW) do regarding the truth he was commanded to proclaim?
questions.answer: He is told to 'Proclaim what you are commanded' and turn away from the polytheists.

questions.text: How does Allah promise to handle those who mock the Prophet?
questions.answer: 'Indeed, We are sufficient for you against the mockers.'

questions.text: How does Allah acknowledge the Prophet's emotional state regarding the mockers' words?
questions.answer: He says: 'We already know that your breast is constrained by what they say.'

questions.text: What is the remedy given to the Prophet to ease his heart?
questions.answer: Exalt Allah with praise and be among those who prostrate.

questions.text: Until when is the believer commanded to worship Allah?
questions.answer: Until 'Al-Yaqin' (the certainty), which refers to the time of death.

questions.text: What is the 'piercing flame' (Shihab)?
questions.answer: A shooting star or celestial fire that drives away the devils.

questions.text: What is the 'proper balance' in which things grow?
questions.answer: The ecological and biological harmony established by Allah in nature.

questions.text: What is the 'altered black mud'?
questions.answer: A stage in the creation of man from the earth.

questions.text: What is the 'scorching fire' (Nar-is-Samum)?
questions.answer: The intense, smokeless fire from which Jinn were created.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of the time well-known'?
questions.answer: The Day of Resurrection.

questions.text: What is 'Mukhlaseen'?
questions.answer: Servants of Allah who are pure in their faith and selected for His mercy.

questions.text: What is the 'resentment' (Ghill) that is removed in Paradise?
questions.answer: Any negative feelings or traces of hatred between the hearts of the believers.

questions.text: What are the 'springs' of Paradise?
questions.answer: Fountains of pure water and other delights for the inhabitants.

questions.text: What is the 'glad tidings' (Bushra) given to Ibrahim?
questions.answer: The promise of a son who would be a person of knowledge.

questions.text: What is the 'shriek' (Saihah)?
questions.answer: A powerful sound or vibration used as a means of divine punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Rocky Tract' (Al-Hijr)?
questions.answer: The area where the people of Thamud lived, known for its stone-carved buildings.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Creation' (Al-Khallaq)?
questions.answer: An attribute of Allah, the All-Knowing Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Quran' (Al-Quran al-'Azim)?
questions.answer: The magnificent revelation given to the Prophet.

questions.text: What are the 'parts' (Idien) into which some people divided the Quran?
questions.answer: Sectarian interpretations or selective acceptance of the message.

questions.text: What is the 'Proclamation' (Isda')?
questions.answer: Declaring the message of Islam openly and clearly.

questions.text: What is the 'constrained breast'?
questions.answer: The feeling of sadness and pressure the Prophet felt from the rejection of his people.

questions.text: What is the 'certainty' (Al-Yaqin)?
questions.answer: Death, after which all doubts are removed and reality is seen.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention the constellations as 'beautified'?
questions.answer: To draw attention to the artistic and orderly nature of His creation.

questions.text: What is the 'depositories' (Khaza'in)?
questions.answer: The unlimited resources and treasures of everything that Allah controls.

questions.text: What is the 'fertilizing' wind?
questions.answer: Wind that carries seeds or causes the formation of rain.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention humans being created from 'altered black mud'?
questions.answer: To remind humans of humble origins and Allah’s power to create and resurrect, cutting arrogance and denial.

questions.text: Why did Iblis focus on the 'mud'?
questions.answer: To justify his arrogance by looking only at the physical shell rather than the divine soul.

questions.text: What is the 'curse' on Iblis?
questions.answer: His permanent distance from the mercy of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'authority' of Iblis?
questions.answer: He has no power to force anyone; he only has influence over those who choose to follow him.

questions.text: What is the 'assigned group' (Juz'un Maqsum)?
questions.answer: The specific division of sinners for each gate of Hell.

questions.text: What is the 'brotherhood' of Paradise?
questions.answer: A state of perfect social and spiritual harmony among the believers.

questions.text: What is the 'throne' (Surur)?
questions.answer: The raised seats of honor in Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'learned son'?
questions.answer: Prophet Ishaq (Isaac).

questions.text: Why did Lut offer his 'daughters'?
questions.answer: To protect his guests and to encourage a lawful and natural way of life for his people.

questions.text: What is the 'turning upside down' of the city?
questions.answer: The physical destruction of the land of Sodom.

questions.text: What is the 'sign for those who discern' (Mutawassimin)?
questions.answer: The ruins of the destroyed nations left as lessons for those who reflect.

questions.text: What is the 'clear way' (Imamin mubin) mentioned regarding Madyan?
questions.answer: The remains of the city that people still pass by on their journeys.

questions.text: What is the 'security' the people of Al-Hijr thought they had?
questions.answer: Their strong stone houses which they thought would protect them from any disaster.

questions.text: What is the 'coming of the Hour'?
questions.answer: The approach of the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is the 'pardon' (Al-Safh al-Jamil)?
questions.answer: A beautiful and gracious forgiveness that the Prophet is told to show.

questions.text: What is the 'kindness to believers'?
questions.answer: The humble and compassionate attitude the Prophet is told to have toward his followers.

questions.text: What is the 'glorification' (Tasbih)?
questions.answer: Praising Allah and acknowledging His perfection to find strength.
